findings_blocking: 0
finding: id=R1 scope=pre_existing action=follow_up status=open severity=medium fix_role=none rationale=adaptive-node.js:4522 synthesizeLevel leg-dirty probe catch still collapses to dirty='' (capture skipped) — pre-existing fail-open, NOT introduced/worsened here (this diff only added the 64MB cap, strictly reducing the trigger probability), outside this node's write mandate (plan step 4: only :3253 categorically must flip). Deferral CORRECT, but file the follow-up with corrected exposure: n1's justification cites "legs stay EMPTY in production (S2 dormant-but-tested, ~6256)" — that citation is STALE; adaptive-node.js:7110-7111 states the leg machinery is "LIVE since #542 ... exercised on every disjoint-write co-open by default (no longer dormant)". The silent-loss shape n1 described is real and verified: a probe fault at synthesis (after a successful member-close vacuity probe) omits uncommitted leg content from M, and the leg_omitted_from_merge group-barrier check (plan-validator.js:3181-3197) verifies only branch-resolution + leg-HEAD ancestry of M, never branch content completeness, so it would not catch the omission. Live exposure is low-probability (transient git fault window between member close and synthesis), not nil.
finding: id=R2 scope=pre_existing action=follow_up status=open severity=low fix_role=none rationale=claim.js:483 worktreeDirtyState catch returns 'missing' although fs.existsSync(wtPath) was true one line above — "exists but unprobeable" conflated with "absent". Verified consumer: cmdSweepLegacyWorktrees (claim.js:3455) branches only on state==='dirty' for skip/stash protection, so a probe fault on a real possibly-dirty worktree routes to would_remove/removal. Pre-existing fail-open on a destructive path; this diff's cap removes the ENOBUFS trigger, leaving only genuine git faults. Adding a new refusal mode to a shipped destructive path is a value-laden scope call — deferral CORRECT.

# n2-review (G1 code-reviewer gate) — issue #669 ENOBUFS hardening of the `git status --porcelain` family

Reviewed: 17-file diff (scripts/{plan-validator,adaptive-node,claim,sink-merge,test-adaptive-node}.js + codex twins + gitlab/gitea forge ports), against n1-porcelain-cap evidence and the frozen plan mandate.

## (a) Completeness of the cap — PASS
Grepped all 16 edited production scripts for `status', '--porcelain` exec sites: 42 hits, every one carries `maxBuffer: GIT_MAX_BUFFER`. The 3 apparent misses from a same-line grep (claim.js:483/:1634, adaptive-node.js:5499 + their edition mirrors) are multi-line calls with maxBuffer on the continuation line — verified by reading each. Site counts match n1's ledger exactly: canonical 10 sites (plan-validator:3267; sink-merge:147/223/984; adaptive-node:4522/5484/5499; claim:483/532/1634), gitlab claim 293/494/1402 + sink-merge 160/228/822/1041, gitea claim 294/494/1403 + sink-merge 159/227/816/1035, codex twins byte-identical. `GIT_MAX_BUFFER = 64*1024*1024` (from #666) confirmed defined in every hand-edited file. Converse held: 26 `git worktree list --porcelain` sites repo-wide, ZERO capped. Out-of-mandate observation (non-blocking): closure-audit.js:166/173 and release.js:44 are also porcelain content probes and remain uncapped — but both catches are already fail-CLOSED (`return true` = dirty; `{ok:false, reason:'worktree_status_unavailable'}`), so an ENOBUFS there refuses rather than passes; they are outside the issue's and plan's enumerated site list.

## (b) Fail-closed is genuine — PASS
Read the full post-fix `--parent-clean-check` handler (plan-validator.js:3229-3295): the catch emits the typed `{result:'refuse', reason:'cannot_prove_clean', operator_hint, errors}` JSON, sets `process.exitCode = 1`, and `return`s immediately — no path from the catch to the pass branch; all four handler exits (root_mismatch / cannot_prove_clean / parent_dirty / pass) terminate with `return`. `cannot_prove_clean` is registered in OPERATOR_HINT_REGISTRY (:151). Consumer claim verified by reading both consumers: `parentCarriesProductionDirt` (adaptive-node.js:4263-4269) returns `fence.exitCode !== 0 || fence.result !== 'pass'` — any refuse classifies as dirt → serial degrade; the last-member close fence (adaptive-node.js:5904-5907) refuses on the same literal predicate and propagates `fence.reason` — the new reason integrates with zero consumer changes, exactly as n1 claimed. n1's per-site fail-safety justifications for the untouched catches verified sound: memberInLaneChanges 5484/5499 `{changed:false}` feeds the member_vacuity REFUSE at runCloseNode (probe failure forces an explicit no_op declaration or a refusal, never a silent close); sink-merge :147/:984 have no catch (fail-closed-by-crash, callers documented zero-mutation); :223 throws a typed probeErr per its #496 comment (unverifiable = dirty); claim :532/:1634 `catch → true` are the documented #557/#563 fail-closed direction.

## (c) Cross-edition integrity — PASS
Ran `node scripts/edition-sync.js --check` myself: "edition-sync: 10 forge aggregator ports, 24 COMMON_SCRIPTS mirrors, and 27 byte-identical groups in parity with canonical." — clean. The two GENERATED aggregators regenerated (forge-port line offsets +1 only), and the DIVERGENT gitlab/gitea claim/sink-merge hand-ports match the canonical cap pattern site-for-site (including gitlab's fork-local extra inline -uno site at :822 / gitea :816, correctly capped).

## (d) Real RED, not vacuous — PASS
Fixture math verified from the test source: porcelain line = "?? " (3) + relpath 206 chars (src/generated/ 14 + 60a + / + 60b + / + 60c + / + f#####.js 9) + newline = 210 B x 6000 files = 1,260,000 B > the 1,048,576 B execFileSync default — the fixture genuinely exceeds 1 MB. Paths sit under src/generated/… — production, out-of-allowband (NOT kaola-workflow/**), so the fence must flag them; against the unpatched fence the ENOBUFS collapses to porcelain='' → false pass, exactly the RED n1 recorded (2 failures / 1767 passed). Assertions are typed, not merely non-pass: `result==='refuse' && (reason==='parent_dirty'||'cannot_prove_clean') && exitCode!==0`. Discrimination against a masked fix (cap added, catch still empties): the ENOBUFS fixture alone would NOT catch it (1.26 MB now fits under the 64 MB cap → probe succeeds → parent_dirty), but the paired PARENT-CLEAN-CHECK-CANNOT-PROVE-CLEAN regression (corrupt .git/index — a size-independent git fault) forces the catch path and asserts `reason==='cannot_prove_clean' && exitCode!==0`, which the masked fix fails (it would emit a bare pass). The PAIR is therefore discriminating; the only unpinned variant is flip-without-cap, which is the safe (fail-closed, noisier) direction. The harness-side runVal maxBuffer raise is test-only (scripts/test-adaptive-node.js) and does not mask the production probe under test — it widens the harness's own capture of the (legitimately large) parent_dirty JSON response.

## (e) Four-chain obligation — CONFIRMED STANDING; local suites green
The diff touches plugins/kaola-workflow-{gitlab,gitea}/scripts/ → genuinely cross-edition → all four `npm run test:kaola-workflow:{claude,codex,gitlab,gitea}` chains (run sequentially) are REQUIRED before Finalization; not run here (finalize owns them). Ran locally: `node scripts/test-adaptive-node.js` → "adaptive-node tests passed (1770 assertions)" (matches n1's post-fix count = 1767 + 3 new); `node scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js` → "Workflow walkthrough simulation passed", exit 0. The pre-existing #671 EISDIR workflow-tasks.json stderr trace appeared identically (subprocess fixture, unrelated, matches n1's note).

## (f) The two residuals — deferral CORRECT for both (findings R1/R2 above, non-blocking)
Neither is a regression this change introduced or worsened — both empty catches predate the diff and the added cap strictly reduces their trigger probability. Both sit outside the node's write mandate; the plan's step 4 asked n1 to "decide and justify", which it did. R1 (adaptive-node.js:4522): deferral upheld, but n1's "live exposure effectively nil / S2 dormant" support is stale (leg provisioning LIVE since #542 per adaptive-node.js:7110) — the follow-up should be filed at live-but-low-probability exposure, with the verified leg_omitted_from_merge blind spot (ancestry-only, no content-completeness check). R2 (claim.js:483): deferral upheld; the 'missing' conflation feeds a destructive sweep consumer but is pre-existing and now ENOBUFS-immune.

## Verdict
APPROVE. verdict: pass, findings_blocking: 0. The crux flip is genuine and unconditional, the cap sweep is complete over the mandated family, editions are in parity, the regression pair is discriminating, and both deferred residuals are correctly out of scope (with one evidence-accuracy correction for the follow-up filing).
